title: The bakery that opens at four
summary: A small portrait of early-morning bakers and the people who buy the first bread.
topic: The bakery that opens at four
prompt: Write 8–10 sentences (150–250 words) about a workplace that opens very early or closes very late. Describe the workers and the first or last customers. Use past simple, past continuous, and 'when'.
hintStarters: On the corner of my street in…, there is a bakery that opens at…
When I walked past one cold morning at four, I saw…
While the baker was pulling a tray of bread out of the oven, he…
sampleText: On the corner of my street in Lyon, there is a small bakery that opens at four in the morning. One cold winter night I could not sleep, so at half past three I walked past it on the way to buy milk. The lights were already on, and through the window I saw two men in flour-covered aprons. One was kneading a huge piece of dough on a long wooden table, and the other was sliding a metal tray out of the big black oven. The smell that came out when the door opened was the warmest thing in the city. I stopped and watched for a few minutes. When the older baker noticed me, he smiled and waved me inside. He gave me a small piece of fresh bread, still hot, and refused to take any money. While he was wrapping more loaves for the morning delivery, he told me he had worked in that bakery for thirty-eight years and had never been late once. I learned that night that some jobs start in the dark and finish before most people open their eyes. I walked home with the warm bread in my hand and forgot completely about the milk.

mặc tạp dề dính đầy bột

sampleAnnotations.phrase: kneading a huge piece of dough
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: đang nhào một khối bột lớn

sampleAnnotations.phrase: sliding a metal tray out of the big black oven
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: đang rút khay kim loại ra khỏi cái lò nướng đen lớn

sampleAnnotations.phrase: the warmest thing in the city
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: thứ ấm áp nhất trong cả thành phố
vẫy tay gọi tôi vào trong

sampleAnnotations.phrase: While he was wrapping more loaves
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: Trong khi ông đang gói thêm những ổ bánh mì
chưa từng đi muộn một lần nào
hoàn toàn quên mất chuyện mua sữa

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Past perfect for earlier-than-past
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: 'Had + V3' diễn tả việc xảy ra trước một mốc thời gian khác trong quá khứ: 'he had worked in that bakery for thirty-eight years and had never been late once.' Đây là điều ông kể tại thời điểm gặp tôi (quá khứ).
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: 'Had + past participle' marks something that happened before another past point: 'he had worked in that bakery for thirty-eight years and had never been late once.' This refers to a period before the moment of meeting.

sampleGrammarNotes.title: 'When' + past simple, past simple
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Hai mệnh đề past simple nối bằng 'when' diễn tả hai sự kiện xảy ra liên tiếp: 'When the older baker noticed me, he smiled and waved me inside.'
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: Two past-simple clauses joined by 'when' show two events one right after another: 'When the older baker noticed me, he smiled and waved me inside.'

sampleTranslationVi: Ở góc phố của tôi tại Lyon, có một tiệm bánh nhỏ mở cửa lúc bốn giờ sáng. Một đêm đông lạnh tôi không ngủ được, nên lúc ba giờ rưỡi tôi đi qua nó trên đường mua sữa. Đèn đã bật sáng, và qua cửa sổ tôi thấy hai người đàn ông mặc tạp dề dính đầy bột. Một người đang nhào một khối bột lớn trên chiếc bàn gỗ dài, và người kia đang rút khay kim loại ra khỏi cái lò nướng đen lớn. Mùi thơm bay ra khi cánh cửa mở là thứ ấm áp nhất trong cả thành phố. Tôi dừng lại và xem trong vài phút. Khi người thợ làm bánh lớn tuổi hơn nhận ra tôi, ông mỉm cười và vẫy tay gọi tôi vào trong. Ông đưa cho tôi một miếng bánh mì tươi, vẫn còn nóng, và từ chối nhận tiền. Trong khi ông đang gói thêm những ổ bánh mì cho đợt giao hàng buổi sáng, ông kể với tôi rằng ông đã làm việc trong tiệm bánh đó ba mươi tám năm và chưa từng đi muộn một lần nào. Đêm đó tôi học được rằng một số công việc bắt đầu trong bóng tối và kết thúc trước khi hầu hết mọi người mở mắt. Tôi đi bộ về nhà với ổ bánh mì ấm trên tay và hoàn toàn quên mất chuyện mua sữa.

options: a small bakery that opens at four in the morning
a small bakery who opens at four in the morning
a small bakery what opens at four in the morning
a small bakery whose opens at four in the morning
mcQuestions.explanation: 'That' (or 'which') is used for things; 'who' is for people.

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the correct 'while' + past continuous sentence.
options: While he wrapped more loaves, he told me about his job.
While he was wrapping more loaves, he told me about his job.
While he was wrap more loaves, he told me about his job.
While he is wrapping more loaves, he told me about his job.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'While + past continuous' for the background; past simple for the event.

options: He had worked there for thirty-eight years.
He has worked there for thirty-eight years.
He worked there for thirty-eight years and never has been late.
He was working there for thirty-eight years and never been late.
mcQuestions.explanation: Past perfect 'had worked' fits an experience reported within a past story.
mcQuestions.hint: Had + V3 inside a past narrative.

options: the soft mixture of flour and water before baking
the brown crust on top of bread
the metal tray for bread
the apron of a baker

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct meaning of 'wave someone inside'.
options: to invite someone in with a hand gesture
to push someone out the door
to ignore someone in the street
to wash someone's hands
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Wave inside' = invite by a hand motion.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct sentence with 'refuse + to + verb'.
options: He refused to take any money.
He refused taking any money.
He refused for take any money.
He refused take any money.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Refuse + to + base verb' is the standard pattern.

options: a single shaped piece of bread
a slice of bread already cut
a small biscuit
a sandwich
mcQuestions.explanation: A 'loaf' is one whole shaped piece of bread.

options: the warm-er-est thing in the city
the warmer thing in the city
the warmest thing in the city
the most warm thing in the city

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the correct word order with 'never'.
options: He had never been late once.
He had been never late once.
He never had been late once.
He had been late never once.
mcQuestions.explanation: Adverb 'never' goes between 'had' and the past participle.

options: to move something to a new place
a scheduled period of work
a kind of bread
a delivery van
mcQuestions.explanation: Noun 'shift' = a period when someone works (morning shift, night shift).

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the most natural sentence using 'on the way to'.
options: I walked past it on the way for buy milk.
I walked past it on the way to buy milk.
I walked past it on way to buy milk.
I walked past it on the way I buy milk.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'On the way to + base verb' (purpose) is the natural pattern.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Why do some workers, like bakers or street cleaners, stay loyal to a difficult schedule for decades? What might keep them coming back?
